popularStory.headline: Vladimir the Great (also (Saint) Vladimir of Kiev; Old Norse Valdamarr gamli; c. 958-15 July 1015, Berestove)
popularStory.description: Vladimir the Great (also (Saint) Vladimir of Kiev; Old Norse Valdamarr gamli; c. 958-15 July 1015, Berestove) was a prince of Novgorod, grand prince of Kiev, and ruler of Kievan Rus' from 980 to 1015. …

zh.Berestovo, Kharkiv, Ukraine: 烏克蘭Харківська областьЗміївський район貝雷斯托沃
zh.lifeSummary: 當Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev於0960年出生在烏克蘭Волинська時，他的父親Sviatoslav I Igorevich17歲，母親Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev26歲。他於大約0978年與Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev在Российская империяКиевская губернияКиевский уездКиїв結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1015年7月15日在烏克蘭Харківська областьЗміївський район貝雷斯托沃享年55歲，埋葬在烏克蘭Київ。

zh-hans.Berestovo, Kharkiv, Ukraine: 乌克兰Харківська областьЗміївський район貝雷斯托沃
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev于0960年出生在乌克兰Волинська时，他的父亲Sviatoslav I Igorevich17岁，母亲Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev26岁。他于大约0978年与Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev在Российская империяКиевская губернияКиевский уездКиїв结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1015年7月15日在乌克兰Харківська областьЗміївський район貝雷斯托沃享年55岁，埋葬在乌克兰Київ。

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Prinz ‘prince’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Grand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: nickname from Old French grand, grant ‘tall, large’, from Latin grandis. See also Grant 1 and 4, compare Degrand and Legrand .
German: topographic name for someone who lived in a gravelly area, from Middle Low German grand ‘coarse gravel’.
German: nickname from a southern word of uncertain origin meaning ‘frustration, anger, ire’.

variants.name: Grant
meanings: Irish, English, and especially Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French graund, graunt ‘tall, large’ (Old French grand, grant, from Latin grandis), given either to a person of remarkable size, or else in a relative way to distinguish two bearers of the same personal name, often representatives of different generations within the same family.
English: from the rare Middle English (and Old English) personal name Grante or Grente.
Irish: in Ireland this is usually the Norman Scottish name (see 1 above), but it was also adopted for Irish Mag Raighne, see Graney .

variants.name: Degrand
meanings: French: patronymic, with fused preposition de ‘of’, from the nickname Grand ‘tall, large’.

American shortened form of Walloon Degrandgagnage, itself a variant, with fused preposition de ‘from’, of Grandgagnage, a habitational name from Grandgagnage in Wallonia (Belgium). The surname Degrandgagnage is not found in Belgium.

variants.name: Grandin
meanings: French: nickname from a diminutive of   Grand   ‘big, tall’.
Italian: from a derivative of   Grande  .

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Prinz ‘prince’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Kiev
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Austrian German: occupational name for a cowherd, Chüyger in the Tyrolean dialect, from Kühe ‘cows’ (plural of Kuh) + -er suffix of agent nouns.
English (Lancashire): variant of   Kear  .
Scottish (Ayrshire and Lanarkshire): variant of   Keir  .

variants.name: Kear
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): occupational name for a locksmith, from Middle English keyer, kayer ‘key-maker, locksmith’ (Old English cǣgere, a derivative of cǣg ‘key’).

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Great
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Margareta, a Latinate form of Margaret . As an independent given name it became fairly popular in the English-speaking world as a result of the fame of the Swedish-born film actress Greta Garbo ( 1905–92 , born Greta Louisa Gustafsson ).

variants.name: Gretta
meanings: Short form of Margareta, a Latinate form of Margaret . As an independent given name it became fairly popular in the English-speaking world as a result of the fame of the Swedish-born film actress Greta Garbo ( 1905–92 , born Greta Louisa Gustafsson ).

historicEvents.description: Prince Svyatoslav makes peace with Byzantine Empire.
Prince Vladimir converts Kievan Rus' to Christianity.
Prince Vladimir's death leads Rurik princes into fratricidal war that continues until 1036.

en.lifeSummary: When Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev was born in 0960, in Volyn, Ukraine, his father, Sviatoslav I Igorevich, was 17 and his mother, Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev, was 26. He married Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev about 0978, in Kyiv, Kiev Uyezd, Kiev, Russian Empire. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 15 July 1015, in Berestovo, Zmiiv, Kharkiv, Ukraine, at the age of 55, and was buried in Kyiv, Ukraine.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev est né en 0960, à Volyn, Ukraine, son père, Sviatoslav I Igorevich, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev environ 0978, à Kyiv, Kyyivsʹkyy Povit, Kiev, Empire russe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 15 juillet 1015, à Berestovo, Zmiiv, Kharkiv, Ukraine, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Kyiv, Ukraine.

de.lifeSummary: Als Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev 0960 in Volyn, Ukraine geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sviatoslav I Igorevich 17 und seine Mutter Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev 26. Er hat um 0978 in Kiew, Kyyivsʹkyy Povit, Kiev, Russisches Kaiserreich Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 15. Juli 1015 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Berestovo, Zmiiv, Kharkiv, Ukraine und wurde in Kyiv, Ukraine begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev è nato il nel 0960 nel Volyn, Ucraina, il suo padre, Sviatoslav I Igorevich, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev circa 0978, nel Kyiv, Kyyivsʹkyy Povit, Kiev, Russisches Kaiserreich. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 15 luglio 1015, nel Berestovo, Zmiiv, Kharkiv, Ucraina, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Kyiv, Ucraina.

ja.lifeSummary: Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kievが0960年にウクライナВолинськаで生まれた時，父Sviatoslav I Igorevichは17歳，母Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kievは26歳であった。約0978年にРоссийская империяКиевская губернияКиевский уездКиївでPrincess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kievと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1015年7月15日にウクライナХарківська областьЗміївський районバーエストボで55歳で死去し，ウクライナКиївにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev(이)가 0960년 우크라이나 Волинська에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sviatoslav I Igorevich(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 0978년 Российская империя Киевская губерния Киевский уезд Київ에서 Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1015년 7월 15일 우크라이나 Харківська область Зміївський район 버스토보에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 우크라이나 Київ에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev nasceu em 0960, em Volyn, Ucrânia, seu pai, Sviatoslav I Igorevich, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev aproximadamente 0978, em Kyiv, Kyyivsʹkyy Povit, Kiev, Russisches Kaiserreich. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 15 de julho de 1015, em Berestovo, Zmiiv, Kharkiv, Ucrânia, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Kyiv, Ucrânia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Volodymyr I  the Great Volodymr Svyatoslavich Prince of Novgorod Grand Prince of Kiev родился в 0960 г. в местности Волынская, Украина, его отцу по имени Sviatoslav I Igorevich было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Malusha Malkovna de Lubeck Grand Duchess of Kiev, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev около 0978 г. в местности Киев, Киевский уезд, Киевская губерния, Российская империя. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 15 июля 1015 г. в местности бирестово, Змиевской район, Харьковская область, Украина в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности Киев, Украина.

Se casó con Princess Olava (Allogia) Eriksdottir of Norway, Weden and Bohemia,Grand Princess of Kiev aproximadamente 0978, en Kyiv, Kyyivsʹkyy Povit, Kiev, Russisches Kaiserreich.
